ab: koza konako si tamali amwanai lika’e agi tamali ka sawalay.
ch: 昨天我和爸爸去拜訪我的叔叔。
en: Yesterday, my father and I visited my uncle.

ab: madola dra’ale agi ki tamali ka sawalay.
kwani elaela naiya , daane calraviya matrabelrang eea ka barongolo
ch: 他告訴我們，房子的屋頂破了一個洞。
en: He told us that there was a hole in the roof of his house.

ab: kwani sa ’odringadringi calraviya, twalai calravaya mocili mwdaedae.
ch: 他在修理屋頂時，從屋頂摔下來了。
en: He fell off the roof while repairing it.

ab: tamali wailoko bwate ki goong si sabiki baai ki agi ki tamali ka sawalay.
ch: 爸爸帶了牛肉和檳榔給叔叔。
en: My father brought beef and betel nuts for my uncle.

ab: konako si agi ki tamale ka sawaly ikai daane masosososo. Kawni akiso’ili sa makalra maolai
ch: 我和叔叔在房間裡聊天，他分享了很多故事。
en: My uncle and I chatted in the room, and he shared many stories.

ab: sa aikiovagaizako, agi tamali ka sawalay osamalra nakoa mabelengnga , si haro aniomasa miya.
ch: 我要離開前，叔叔誇我長高了，也變得更帥氣了。
en: Before I left, my uncle praised me for growing taller and becoming more handsome.

ab: la kawrivako, “nama ai adravako dra’aleso modringainga, lo ela niyamanga naiyana kivaevang.”
ch: 我說：「等叔叔腳好了，下次也來我們家玩。」
en: I said, “When your foot gets better, come to our house next time and visit us.”
